L1.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in the API layer. When processing OAuth-authenticated requests, the server only verifies that the user is authenticated and holds the appropriate role (e.g., administrator status), but does not validate that the OAuth access token was actually granted the scopes required for the requested action. Additionally, the token response always reports full permissions to the client regardless of the scopes that were originally authorized. This allows an attacker who obtains an access token with minimal read-only permissions to perform privileged write operations, administrative actions, and other sensitive API calls that should require elevated OAuth scopes.

ground_truth.reason: The API endpoints only check user authentication and admin role but never verify OAuth token scopes, allowing any valid access token (even read-only) to perform privileged operations including administrative actions.
